title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 16: Numbers and Shapes
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many stars are drawn in the picture?
questions.image_description: Five outlined stars in a row.
questions.solution: Counting the stars from left to right gives 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. So there are 5 stars.

questions.question_text: Lina has 4 red balloons and 3 blue balloons. How many balloons does she have altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='90' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='130' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='155' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='10'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Seven balloon circles shown in two groups of 4 and 3.
questions.solution: Adding 4 and 3 gives 4 + 3 = 7 balloons in total.

questions.question_text: Which of these shapes has exactly three sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 250 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><polygon points='70,45 85,15 100,45'/><circle cx='135' cy='30' r='15'/><polygon points='175,15 195,15 200,45 170,45'/><polygon points='225,15 245,30 235,48 215,48 210,30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five shapes: square, triangle, circle, trapezoid, pentagon.
questions.solution: A triangle is the shape with exactly three sides.

questions.question_text: Which number is the largest?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' font-size='20' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='10' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='50' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>21</text><text x='90' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>18</text><text x='130' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text><text x='165' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>15</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Five numbers displayed in a row: 12, 21, 18, 9, 15.
questions.solution: Comparing 12, 21, 18, 9, 15, the biggest number is 21.

questions.question_text: What shape comes next in the pattern: circle, square, circle, square, circle, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='50' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='100' cy='25' r='12'/><rect x='130' y='13' width='24' height='24'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='12'/><text x='215' y='32' font-size='24' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Pattern of alternating circles and squares ending with a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates circle and square. After a circle, the next shape is a square.

questions.question_text: What number is missing: 10 - ? = 6?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='none' font-size='22' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='20' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>10</text><text x='55' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>-</text><rect x='75' y='12' width='28' height='28'/><text x='115' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>=</text><text x='145' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Subtraction equation with a box where the missing number goes.
questions.solution: Since 10 minus 4 equals 6, the missing number is 4.

questions.question_text: How many small triangles can you count in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='100,10 40,90 160,90'/><line x1='70' y1='50' x2='130' y2='50'/><line x1='100' y1='10' x2='100' y2='90'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A big triangle divided into four smaller triangles.
questions.solution: The two internal lines split the big triangle into exactly 4 small triangles.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Sam. Who is the shortest?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='20' height='60'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='20' height='40'/><rect x='120' y='55' width='20' height='25'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars of decreasing height labeled by position.
questions.solution: Tom is tallest, Ben is in the middle, and Sam is the shortest.

questions.question_text: There are 8 apples in a basket. Mia eats 2 and her brother eats 3. How many apples remain?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='45' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='120' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='145' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='170' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='195' cy='30' r='10'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Eight circles representing apples in a row.
questions.solution: Starting with 8 apples and removing 2 + 3 = 5 eaten, leaves 8 - 5 = 3 apples.

questions.question_text: How many corners does the shape below have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 100,40 85,90 35,90 20,40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A five-sided closed figure (pentagon).
questions.solution: The figure is a pentagon, which has 5 corners.

questions.question_text: The numbers follow a rule: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?. What number fills the blank?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='none' font-size='22' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='10' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='50' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4</text><text x='90' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='130' y='32' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>8</text><rect x='170' y='12' width='30' height='28'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Number sequence 2, 4, 6, 8 followed by an empty box.
questions.solution: Each number increases by 2, so after 8 comes 8 + 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: In the grid, how many shaded squares are there?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='35' y='10' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='10' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='85' y='10' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='10' y='35' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='35' y='35' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='60' y='35' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='85' y='35' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='10' y='60' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='35' y='60' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/><rect x='85' y='60' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='10' y='85' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='35' y='85' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='60' y='85' width='25' height='25'/><rect x='85' y='85' width='25' height='25' fill='currentColor'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 4 by 4 grid with some squares shaded in a checker-like pattern.
questions.solution: Counting the shaded squares in the grid gives a total of 7.

questions.question_text: A box has 2 red, 3 green, and 4 yellow marbles. Which color has the most marbles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='80' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='100' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='120' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='200' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='220' cy='30' r='8'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Groups of circles showing 2, 3, and 4 marbles by color group.
questions.solution: Comparing 2, 3, and 4, the largest group is yellow with 4 marbles.

questions.question_text: What is the sum 5 + 5 + 5?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='none' font-size='22' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='20' y='38' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5 + 5 + 5 = ?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: The expression 5 plus 5 plus 5 with a question mark.
questions.solution: Adding step by step, 5 + 5 = 10 and 10 + 5 = 15.

questions.question_text: Which list shows numbers ordered from smallest to largest?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2' fill='none' font-size='18' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='10' y='30' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Compare: 7, 3, 11, 5</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A line of numbers 7, 3, 11, 5 to be ordered.
questions.solution: Sorted from smallest to largest, the numbers are 3, 5, 7, 11.

questions.question_text: Which figure is made only from squares?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='45' cy='25' r='10'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='92' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='165,35 175,15 185,35'/><rect x='215' y='15' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='250,35 260,15 270,35'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Several small figures made from combinations of squares, circles, and triangles.
questions.solution: Only the figure with two squares side by side uses squares alone.

questions.question_text: Anna, Beth, and Cleo sit in a line. Anna is not at an end. Beth is to the left of Anna. Who sits at the far right?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='130' y='20' width='40' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three empty chairs in a row to represent seating positions.
questions.solution: Anna is in the middle. Beth must be to Anna's left, so Beth is on the far left. That leaves Cleo at the far right.

questions.question_text: How many squares of any size are in the 2 by 2 grid of small squares?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='40' height='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A 2 by 2 arrangement of four small squares.
questions.solution: There are 4 small squares plus the 1 large outer square, giving 4 + 1 = 5 squares in total.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 3 coins and an eraser costs 2 coins. Eli buys 2 pencils and 1 eraser. How many coins does he pay?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 70' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='40' height='10'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='40' height='10'/><rect x='120' y='15' width='25' height='20'/><circle cx='180' cy='25' r='10'/><circle cx='205' cy='25' r='10'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Two pencils, one eraser, and sample coins.
questions.solution: Two pencils cost 2 x 3 = 6 coins, and one eraser is 2 coins. Total is 6 + 2 = 8 coins.

questions.question_text: Shapes repeat as triangle, circle, circle, triangle, circle, circle, ... What is the 8th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 320 50' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='15,35 30,10 45,35'/><circle cx='70' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='105' cy='25' r='12'/><polygon points='130,35 145,10 160,35'/><circle cx='185' cy='25' r='12'/><circle cx='220' cy='25' r='12'/><polygon points='245,35 260,10 275,35'/><text x='290' y='32' font-size='22' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></g></svg>
questions.image_description: The repeating pattern triangle, circle, circle shown across seven positions with the eighth marked by a question mark.
questions.solution: The block of 3 repeats: positions 1,4,7 are triangles and 2,3,5,6,8,9 are circles. So the 8th shape is a circle.

questions.question_text: Four kangaroos share 12 carrots equally. How many carrots does each kangaroo get?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='30' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='50' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='90' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='110' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='130' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='150' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='170' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='190' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='210' y='20' width='15' height='30'/><rect x='225' y='20' width='12' height='30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Twelve carrot-shaped rectangles to be shared among four.
questions.solution: Sharing 12 equally among 4 kangaroos, each gets 12 / 4 = 3 carrots.

questions.question_text: Which figure can be folded along the dashed line so that the two halves match exactly?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='20'/><line x1='30' y1='15' x2='30' y2='65' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><polygon points='80,60 110,20 140,60'/><line x1='110' y1='15' x2='110' y2='65' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><polygon points='170,20 210,20 200,60 175,55'/><line x1='190' y1='15' x2='190' y2='65' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><path d='M240 25 L270 25 L275 45 L255 60 L235 45 Z'/><line x1='255' y1='15' x2='255' y2='65' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Four figures each with a dashed vertical line: a circle, a symmetric triangle, an uneven quadrilateral, and an uneven pentagon.
questions.solution: Only the circle and the symmetric triangle have halves that match exactly across the dashed line.

questions.question_text: Mia is 2 years younger than Leo. Leo is 3 years older than Zoe. If Zoe is 6, how old is Mia?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='30' height='50'/><rect x='130' y='30' width='30' height='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars representing ages of Zoe, Leo, and Mia.
questions.solution: If Zoe is 6, then Leo is 6 + 3 = 9. Mia is 2 younger than Leo, so Mia is 9 - 2 = 7.

questions.question_text: A kangaroo hops along the numbers 1, 2, 3, ... and lands on every third number starting from 3. Which of these numbers does the kangaroo NOT land on?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='290' y2='40'/><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='60' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='180' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='210' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='240' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='270' cy='40' r='4'/><path d='M30 40 Q45 10 60 40' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><path d='M60 40 Q75 10 90 40' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/><path d='M90 40 Q105 10 120 40' stroke-dasharray='3,3'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A number line with dots and dashed arcs indicating hops of size three.
questions.solution: Starting at 3 and hopping by 3 gives 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, ... Ten is not in this list.
